T1566.name: Phishing
T1566.description: Adversaries may send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems.
T1566.detection: Monitor for suspicious email attachments and links

T1566.001.name: Phishing: Spearphishing Attachment
T1566.001.description: Adversaries may send spearphishing emails with a malicious attachment.
T1566.001.detection: Monitor email attachments for known malware signatures
T1566.001.mitigation: Email attachment scanning, user awareness training

T1566.002.name: Phishing: Spearphishing Link
T1566.002.description: Adversaries send spearphishing messages with a malicious link to harvest credentials.
T1566.002.detection: Monitor for clicks on suspicious URLs in emails

T1190.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
T1190.description: Adversaries exploit vulnerabilities in internet-facing applications.
T1190.detection: Monitor web application logs for exploitation attempts

T1059.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
T1059.description: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands.
T1059.detection: Monitor process creation for suspicious script execution

T1059.001.name: PowerShell
T1059.001.description: Adversaries may abuse PowerShell commands and scripts for execution.
T1059.001.detection: PowerShell script block logging, module logging

T1547.001.name: Registry Run Keys / Startup Folder
T1547.001.description: Adversaries may achieve persistence by adding a program to a startup folder or registry run keys.
T1547.001.detection: Monitor Registry for changes to run keys

T1068.name: Exploitation for Privilege Escalation
T1068.description: Adversaries exploit software vulnerabilities to elevate privileges.
T1068.detection: Monitor for unusual process behavior after exploitation

T1003.name: OS Credential Dumping
T1003.description: Adversaries may attempt to dump credentials to obtain account login information.
T1003.detection: Monitor for LSASS access, credential dumping tools

T1021.name: Remote Services
T1021.description: Adversaries may use remote services to move laterally within a network.
T1021.detection: Monitor for unusual remote service usage
T1021.mitigation: MFA, network segmentation, limit remote services

T1041.name: Exfiltration Over C2 Channel
T1041.description: Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over an existing command and control channel.
T1041.detection: Monitor for unusual data volumes over C2 channels

T1486.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
T1486.description: Adversaries may encrypt data on target systems to interrupt availability.
T1486.detection: Monitor for mass file encryption, suspicious file extensions

T1071.name: Application Layer Protocol
T1071.description: Adversaries may communicate using application layer protocols to avoid detection.
T1071.detection: Monitor for unusual protocol usage and traffic patterns

T1110.name: Brute Force
T1110.description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to gain access to accounts.
T1110.detection: Monitor for multiple failed authentication attempts

T1189.name: Drive-by Compromise
T1189.description: Adversaries gain access through a user visiting a compromised website.

T1195.name: Supply Chain Compromise
T1195.description: Adversaries manipulate products or delivery mechanisms prior to receipt.
T1195.detection: Monitor for unexpected changes in software builds
